=== app.py ===
import os
import threading
import datetime
import functools
from flask import (
    Flask, render_template, request, redirect, url_for, session, abort, flash
)

# Utility for concurrency-safe file IO
from utils.file_io import read_file_lines, write_file_lines_atomic

app = Flask(__name__)
app.secret_key = os.getenv('SECRET_KEY', 'devsecret')

DATA_DIR = 'data'

# Thread lock for safe file read/write
file_lock = threading.Lock()

# ----------------------
# Helper functions
# ----------------------

def login_required(f):
    @functools.wraps(f)
    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
        if 'username' not in session:
            return redirect(url_for('login', next=request.url))
        return f(*args, **kwargs)
    return decorated


def parse_pipe_line(line, fields_count):
    parts = line.strip().split('|')
    if len(parts) != fields_count:
        return None
    return [p.strip() for p in parts]


def iso_datetime_now():
    return datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat()


# ----------------------
# Models
# ----------------------

class UserModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'users.txt')
    FIELDS = 3  # username|password|other_fields...

    @staticmethod
    def get_all_users():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(UserModel.FILENAME)
        users = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, UserModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                username = parts[0]
                password = parts[1]
                users[username] = {'username': username, 'password': password}
        return users

    @staticmethod
    def validate_user(username, password):
        users = UserModel.get_all_users()
        if username in users and users[username]['password'] == password:
            return True
        return False


class ArticleModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'articles.txt')
    FIELDS = 7  # article_id|owner|title|category|status|created_at|updated_at

    @staticmethod
    def read_all():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(ArticleModel.FILENAME)
        articles = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, ArticleModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                article_id, owner, title, category, status, created_at, updated_at = parts
                articles[article_id] = {
                    'article_id': article_id,
                    'owner': owner,
                    'title': title,
                    'category': category,
                    'status': status,
                    'created_at': created_at,
                    'updated_at': updated_at
                }
        return articles

    @staticmethod
    def save_all(articles):
        lines = []
        for a in articles.values():
            line = '|'.join([
                a['article_id'], a['owner'], a['title'], a['category'], a['status'], a['created_at'], a['updated_at']
            ])
            lines.append(line)
        with file_lock:
            write_file_lines_atomic(ArticleModel.FILENAME, lines)

    @staticmethod
    def add_article(owner, title, category='Uncategorized'):
        article_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        created_at = iso_datetime_now()
        new_article = {
            'article_id': article_id,
            'owner': owner,
            'title': title,
            'category': category,
            'status': 'draft',
            'created_at': created_at,
            'updated_at': created_at,
        }
        articles = ArticleModel.read_all()
        articles[article_id] = new_article
        ArticleModel.save_all(articles)
        return new_article

    @staticmethod
    def update_article(article_id, title=None, category=None, status=None):
        articles = ArticleModel.read_all()
        if article_id not in articles:
            return None
        article = articles[article_id]
        updated = False
        if title is not None:
            article['title'] = title
            updated = True
        if category is not None:
            article['category'] = category
            updated = True
        if status is not None:
            article['status'] = status
            updated = True
        if updated:
            article['updated_at'] = iso_datetime_now()
            articles[article_id] = article
            ArticleModel.save_all(articles)
        return article


import uuid

class VersionModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'article_versions.txt')
    FIELDS = 6  # version_id|article_id|version_number|content|created_at|status

    @staticmethod
    def read_all():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(VersionModel.FILENAME)
        versions = {}
        by_article = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, VersionModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                version_id, article_id, version_number, content, created_at, status = parts
                version_number = int(version_number)
                v = {
                    'version_id': version_id,
                    'article_id': article_id,
                    'version_number': version_number,
                    'content': content,
                    'created_at': created_at,
                    'status': status
                }
                versions[version_id] = v
                by_article.setdefault(article_id, []).append(v)
        # sort versions by version_number for each article
        for art_id in by_article:
            by_article[art_id].sort(key=lambda x: x['version_number'])
        return versions, by_article

    @staticmethod
    def save_all(versions):
        # versions is dict by version_id
        lines = []
        for v in versions.values():
            line = '|'.join([
                v['version_id'], v['article_id'], str(v['version_number']), v['content'], v['created_at'], v['status']
            ])
            lines.append(line)
        with file_lock:
            write_file_lines_atomic(VersionModel.FILENAME, lines)

    @staticmethod
    def add_version(article_id, content, status='draft'):
        versions, by_article = VersionModel.read_all()
        existing_versions = by_article.get(article_id, [])
        new_version_number = existing_versions[-1]['version_number'] + 1 if existing_versions else 1
        version_id = f"{article_id}_v{new_version_number}"
        created_at = iso_datetime_now()
        version = {
            'version_id': version_id,
            'article_id': article_id,
            'version_number': new_version_number,
            'content': content,
            'created_at': created_at,
            'status': status
        }
        versions[version_id] = version
        VersionModel.save_all(versions)
        return version

class ApprovalModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'approvals.txt')
    FIELDS = 5  # approval_id|version_id|approver|status|timestamp

    @staticmethod
    def read_all():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(ApprovalModel.FILENAME)
        approvals = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, ApprovalModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                approval_id, version_id, approver, status, timestamp = parts
                approvals[approval_id] = {
                    'approval_id': approval_id,
                    'version_id': version_id,
                    'approver': approver,
                    'status': status,
                    'timestamp': timestamp
                }
        return approvals

    @staticmethod
    def save_all(approvals):
        lines = []
        for a in approvals.values():
            line = '|'.join([
                a['approval_id'], a['version_id'], a['approver'], a['status'], a['timestamp']
            ])
            lines.append(line)
        with file_lock:
            write_file_lines_atomic(ApprovalModel.FILENAME, lines)

    @staticmethod
    def add_approval(version_id, approver, status):
        approvals = ApprovalModel.read_all()
        approval_id = f"{version_id}_{approver}"
        timestamp = iso_datetime_now()
        approvals[approval_id] = {
            'approval_id': approval_id,
            'version_id': version_id,
            'approver': approver,
            'status': status,
            'timestamp': timestamp
        }
        ApprovalModel.save_all(approvals)
        return approvals[approval_id]

class CommentModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'comments.txt')
    FIELDS = 6  # comment_id|article_id|version_id|username|content|created_at

    @staticmethod
    def read_all():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(CommentModel.FILENAME)
        comments = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, CommentModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                comment_id, article_id, version_id, username, content, created_at = parts
                comments[comment_id] = {
                    'comment_id': comment_id,
                    'article_id': article_id,
                    'version_id': version_id,
                    'username': username,
                    'content': content,
                    'created_at': created_at
                }
        return comments

    @staticmethod
    def save_all(comments):
        lines = []
        for c in comments.values():
            line = '|'.join([
                c['comment_id'], c['article_id'], c['version_id'], c['username'], c['content'], c['created_at']
            ])
            lines.append(line)
        with file_lock:
            write_file_lines_atomic(CommentModel.FILENAME, lines)

    @staticmethod
    def add_comment(article_id, version_id, username, content):
        comments = CommentModel.read_all()
        comment_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        created_at = iso_datetime_now()
        comment = {
            'comment_id': comment_id,
            'article_id': article_id,
            'version_id': version_id,
            'username': username,
            'content': content,
            'created_at': created_at
        }
        comments[comment_id] = comment
        CommentModel.save_all(comments)
        return comment

class WorkflowModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'workflow_stages.txt')
    FIELDS = 2  # category|stages (comma separated)

    @staticmethod
    def read_all():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(WorkflowModel.FILENAME)
        workflows = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, WorkflowModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                category, stages_str = parts
                stages = [s.strip() for s in stages_str.split(',') if s.strip()]
                workflows[category] = stages
        return workflows

class AnalyticsModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'analytics.txt')
    FIELDS = 6  # article_id|version_id|views|unique_visitors|avg_read_time_seconds|shares

    @staticmethod
    def read_all():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(AnalyticsModel.FILENAME)
        analytics = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, AnalyticsModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                article_id, version_id, views, uv, art, shares = parts
                analytics_key = (article_id, version_id)
                analytics[analytics_key] = {
                    'views': int(views),
                    'unique_visitors': int(uv),
                    'avg_read_time_seconds': float(art),
                    'shares': int(shares)
                }
        return analytics

# ----------------------
# Services
# ----------------------

from approval_service import ApprovalService
from analytics_service import AnalyticsService
from article_service import ArticleService

# ----------------------
# Routes
# ----------------------

@app.route('/')
def root():
    if 'username' in session:
        return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
    return redirect(url_for('login'))

@app.route('/login', methods=['GET','POST'])
def login():
    if request.method == 'GET':
        return render_template('login.html')
    username = request.form.get('username','').strip()
    password = request.form.get('password','').strip()
    if not username or not password:
        flash('Username and password required','error')
        return redirect(url_for('login'))
    if UserModel.validate_user(username, password):
        session['username'] = username
        next_url = request.args.get('next') or url_for('dashboard')
        return redirect(next_url)
    flash('Invalid username or password','error')
    return redirect(url_for('login'))

@app.route('/logout')
@login_required
def logout():
    session.pop('username', None)
    flash('Logged out successfully','info')
    return redirect(url_for('login'))

@app.route('/dashboard')
@login_required
def dashboard():
    username = session['username']
    quick_stats = ArticleService.get_quick_stats(username)
    recent_activity = ArticleService.get_recent_activity(username)
    return render_template('dashboard.html', username=username, quick_stats=quick_stats, recent_activity=recent_activity)

@app.route('/article/create', methods=['GET','POST'])
@login_required
def create_article():
    if request.method == 'GET':
        categories = ArticleService.get_all_categories()
        return render_template('create_article.html', categories=categories)
    # POST
    title = request.form.get('title','').strip()
    content = request.form.get('content','').strip()
    category = request.form.get('category','Uncategorized').strip()
    if not title or not content:
        flash('Title and Content are required','error')
        return redirect(url_for('create_article'))
    username = session['username']
    article = ArticleService.create_article(username, title, content, category)
    flash('Article created successfully','success')
    return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article['article_id']))

@app.route('/article/<article_id>/edit', methods=['GET','POST'])
@login_required
def edit_article(article_id):
    username = session['username']
    article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    if article['owner'] != username:
        abort(403)

    if request.method == 'GET':
        versions = VersionModel.read_all()[1].get(article_id, [])
        return render_template('edit_article.html', article=article, versions=versions)

    # POST
    title = request.form.get('title','').strip()
    content = request.form.get('content','').strip()
    if not title or not content:
        flash('Title and Content are required','error')
        return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id))

    # Update article with new title if changed
    ArticleModel.update_article(article_id, title=title)

    # Save new version
    ArticleService.create_version(article_id, content)

    flash('Article updated with new version','success')
    return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id))

@app.route('/article/<article_id>/versions', methods=['GET'])
@login_required
def article_version_history(article_id):
    article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    versions = VersionModel.read_all()[1].get(article_id, [])
    current_version_num = versions[-1]['version_number'] if versions else 0

    # For comparison, demo pass two latest versions
    compare_versions = versions[-2:] if len(versions) > 1 else versions
    return render_template('version_history.html', article=article, versions=versions, compare_versions=compare_versions)

@app.route('/articles/mine', methods=['GET'])
@login_required
def my_articles():
    username = session['username']
    status_filter = request.args.get('status', None)
    all_articles = ArticleModel.read_all()
    filtered = [a for a in all_articles.values() if a['owner'] == username]
    if status_filter:
        filtered = [a for a in filtered if a['status'] == status_filter]
    statuses = set(a['status'] for a in filtered)
    return render_template('my_articles.html', articles=filtered, statuses=sorted(statuses), selected_status=status_filter)

@app.route('/articles/published', methods=['GET'])
def published_articles():
    category_filter = request.args.get('category', None)
    sort_option = request.args.get('sort', 'created_desc')
    all_articles = ArticleModel.read_all()
    published = [a for a in all_articles.values() if a['status'] == 'published']
    if category_filter:
        published = [a for a in published if a['category'] == category_filter]

    # Sorting demo by created_at descending or ascending
    reverse = True if sort_option.endswith('_desc') else False
    key = None
    if 'created' in sort_option:
        key = lambda x: x['created_at']
    elif 'title' in sort_option:
        key = lambda x: x['title'].lower()
    if key:
        published.sort(key=key, reverse=reverse)

    # Extract categories for filter dropdown
    categories = sorted(set(a['category'] for a in all_articles.values()))

    return render_template('published_articles.html', articles=published, categories=categories, selected_category=category_filter, sort_option=sort_option)

@app.route('/calendar', methods=['GET'])
def content_calendar():
    view_mode = request.args.get('view', 'month')
    # For demo, gather publish dates and events
    all_articles = ArticleModel.read_all()
    versions_by_article = VersionModel.read_all()[1]
    scheduled_events = []
    for article in all_articles.values():
        article_id = article['article_id']
        versions = versions_by_article.get(article_id, [])
        # Use latest approved or published version publish date or similar
        for v in reversed(versions):
            if v['status'] in ('approved','published'):
                # Placeholder: assume publish date stored in content (not defined), use created_at
                scheduled_events.append({
                    'article_id': article_id,
                    'title': article['title'],
                    'date': v['created_at']
                })
                break
    return render_template('content_calendar.html', view_mode=view_mode, events=scheduled_events)

@app.route('/article/<article_id>/analytics', methods=['GET'])
@login_required
def article_analytics(article_id):
    article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    versions = VersionModel.read_all()[1].get(article_id, [])
    analytics_all = AnalyticsModel.read_all()

    # Aggregate analytics by summing across versions
    aggregated = {
        'views': 0,
        'unique_visitors': 0,
        'avg_read_time_seconds': 0.0,
        'shares': 0
    }
    count_versions = 0
    for v in versions:
        key = (article_id, v['version_id'])
        a = analytics_all.get(key)
        if a:
            aggregated['views'] += a['views']
            aggregated['unique_visitors'] += a['unique_visitors']
            aggregated['avg_read_time_seconds'] += a['avg_read_time_seconds']
            aggregated['shares'] += a['shares']
            count_versions += 1
    if count_versions > 0:
        aggregated['avg_read_time_seconds'] /= count_versions

    return render_template('article_analytics.html', article=article, analytics_overview=aggregated)

# Approval submit
@app.route('/article/<article_id>/approve', methods=['POST'])
@login_required
def article_approve(article_id):
    username = session['username']
    article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    status = request.form.get('status','').strip()
    if status not in ('approved', 'rejected', 'revision_requested'):
        flash('Invalid approval status','error')
        return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id))

    versions = VersionModel.read_all()[1].get(article_id, [])
    if not versions:
        flash('No versions found to approve','error')
        return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id))
    latest_version = versions[-1]
    version_id = latest_version['version_id']

    ApprovalModel.add_approval(version_id, username, status)

    # Evaluate aggregate approval status
    ApprovalService.update_version_status(article_id, version_id)

    flash('Approval status updated','success')
    return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))

# Add comment
@app.route('/article/<article_id>/comment', methods=['POST'])
@login_required
def article_comment(article_id):
    username = session['username']
    article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    content = request.form.get('content','').strip()
    version_id = request.form.get('version_id','').strip()
    if not content or not version_id:
        flash('Comment content and version required','error')
        return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))

    CommentModel.add_comment(article_id, version_id, username, content)

    flash('Comment added','success')
    return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))


if __name__ == '__main__':
    os.makedirs(DATA_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    app.run(debug=True)

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Edit Article</title>
</head>
<body>
<div id="edit_article-page">
    <h1>Edit Article</h1>
    <form id="edit_article_form" method="POST" action="{{ url_for('edit_article', article_id=article.id) }}">

article_approve(article_id):     username = session['username']     article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)     if article is None:         abort(404)     status = request.form.get('status','').strip()     if status not in ('approved', 'rejected', 'revision_requested'):         flash('Invalid approval status','error')         return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id))      versions = VersionModel.read_all()[1].get(article_id, [])     if not versions:         flash('No versions found to approve','error')         return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id))     latest_version = versions[-1]     version_id = latest_version['version_id']      ApprovalModel.add_approval(version_id, username, status)      # Evaluate aggregate approval status     ApprovalService.update_version_status(article_id, version_id)      flash('Approval status updated','success')     return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))  # Add comment @app.route('/article/<article_id>/comment', methods=['POST']) @login_required def article_comment(article_id):     username = session['username']     article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)     if article is None:         abort(404)     content = request.form.get('content','').strip()     version_id = request.form.get('version_id','').strip()     if not content or not version_id:         flash('Comment content and version required','error')         return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))      CommentModel.add_comment(article_id, version_id, username, content)      flash('Comment added','success')     return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))   if __name__ == '__main__':     os.makedirs(DATA_DIR, exist_ok=True)     app.run(debug=True)

Defect: Templates Reference Nonexistent or Incorrect Attribute Names\nDescription: Multiple templates reference article or version attributes using incorrect attribute names that do not align with backend dictionary keys.\nExamples:\n- edit_article.html uses article.id and article.content but backend article dictionary uses 'article_id' and no direct 'content' field.\n- my_articles.html uses article.id and article.last_modified, but backend article dict uses 'article_id' and 'updated_at' instead of 'last_modified'.\n- published_articles.html references article.id and article.published_date; backend articles have no 'published_date' field.\n- article_analytics.html references analytics_overview.total_views and avg_time_on_page but backend provides keys as 'views' and 'avg_read_time_seconds'.\n- version_history.html references version.version_id and version.timestamp; backend version dict uses 'version_id' and 'created_at'.\nSteps to Reproduce:\n- Login and navigate to relevant pages such as Edit Article, My Articles, Published Articles, Analytics, or Version History.\n- Observe template rendering errors or missing data display.\nSuggestion:\n- Align template variable usage with backend dictionary keys, e.g., article.article_id, article.updated_at instead of article.id, article.last_modified.\n- Pass additional data if needed, e.g., computed published_date.\n- Modify analytics template to use keys 'views' and 'avg_read_time_seconds'.\n- Ensure consistent naming conventions.\n\n3. Defect: My Articles Page Filter Form Uses Incorrect Parameter Name\nDescription: The my_articles route expects a 'status' query parameter for filtering, but my_articles.html filter form submits parameter named 'filter_status'. This mismatch causes filter not to work.\nSteps to Reproduce:\n- Login and go to /articles/mine\n- Apply filter by status and submit\n- Observe no filtering occurs\nSuggestion:\n- Change filter form select name attribute from 'filter_status' to 'status' to match backend query parameter.\n\n4. Defect: Dashboard's Article Analytics Button uses static article_id=0 causing 404\nDescription: On dashboard.html, the Article Analytics button hardcodes article_id=0 which likely does not exist, leading to 404 error when accessed.\nSteps to Reproduce:\n- Login and land on dashboard\n- Click Article Analytics button\n- Observe 404 error page\nSuggestion:\n- Change the Article Analytics nav button to link dynamically to analytics of a valid article,\n  or disable/replace it with a page listing articles to choose from for analytics.\n\n5. Defect: Article Edit Form Does Not Provide Content Field on GET\nDescription: The edit_article route GET expects to render edit_article.html with article content, but the ArticleModel article dictionary has no 'content' field on article level, content is in versions.\nSteps to Reproduce:\n- Login and navigate to edit an article\n- Observe the content textarea is blank or does not populate\nSuggestion:\n- Populate the content textarea with the latest version's content for that article.\n- Pass latest version content separately to template.\n\n6. Defect: Article Analytics Page Expects traffic_by_date but Backend Does Not Provide\nDescription: The article_analytics.html expects analytics_overview.traffic_by_date to render a table, but backend analytics_overview dict has no such data.\nSteps to Reproduce:\n- Login and go to article analytics page\n- Observe template error for missing 'traffic_by_date' or empty table\nSuggestion:\n- Adjust backend to include dummy or real traffic_by_date data in analytics_overview or\n- Modify template to handle absence gracefully.\n\n7. Defect: Version History Template Has Incorrect Variable Names and Missing Comparison Logic\nDescription: version_history.html template expects 'comparison' variable and fields like version.timestamp, but backend route provides compare_versions list, no comparison variable. Also version dict uses 'created_at' not 'timestamp'.\nSteps to Reproduce:\n- Login and open version history page\n- Observe errors or missing comparison data\nSuggestion:\n- Backend route should prepare a comparison dictionary or object for template.\n- Update template to match version dict keys.\n\n---\n\nFinal Status: NEED_MODIFY\n\nAll above defects prevent full functional usage of the application.\nProviding the missing utils module and aligning template variables to backend data structure, as well as fixing form parameter names and dynamic routing, are essential fixes.\n"})

  [feedback_testing.txt] Feedback for Content Publishing Hub - Functional Testing  1. Defect: Missing utils.file_io Module Causes Import Errors Description: The backend app.py fails to start due to missing 'utils.file_io' module which provides read_file_lines and write_file_lines_atomic functions. This blocks all functionality since models rely on this module for file IO. Steps to Reproduce: - Start or import app.py or any model class - Observe ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'utils' Suggestion: - Provide or restore the utils/file_io.py module containing the required file IO functions. - Alternatively, replace file IO in models with an existing and accessible implementation.  2. Defect: Templates Reference Nonexistent or Incorrect Attribute Names Description: Multiple templates reference article or version attributes using incorrect attribute names that do not align with backend dictionary keys. Examples: - edit_article.html uses article.id and article.content but backend article dictionary uses 'article_id' and no direct 'content' field. - my_articles.html uses article.id and article.last_modified, but backend article dict uses 'article_id' and 'updated_at' instead of 'last_modified'. - published_articles.html references article.id and article.published_date; backend articles have no 'published_date' field. - article_analytics.html references analytics_overview.total_views and avg_time_on_page but backend provides keys as 'views' and 'avg_read_time_seconds'. - version_history.html references version.version_id and version.timestamp; backend version dict uses 'version_id' and 'created_at'. Steps to Reproduce: - Login and navigate to relevant pages such as Edit Article, My Articles, Published Articles, Analytics, or Version History. - Observe template rendering errors or missing data display. Suggestion: - Align template variable usage with backend dictionary keys, e.g., article.article_id, article.updated_at instead of article.id, article.last_modified. - Pass additional data if needed, e.g., computed published_date. - Modify analytics template to use keys 'views' and 'avg_read_time_seconds'. - Ensure consistent naming conventions.  3. Defect: My Articles Page Filter Form Uses Incorrect Parameter Name Description: The my_articles route expects a 'status' query parameter for filtering, but my_articles.html filter form submits parameter named 'filter_status'. This mismatch causes filter not to work. Steps to Reproduce: - Login and go to /articles/mine - Apply filter by status and submit - Observe no filtering occurs Suggestion: - Change filter form select name attribute from 'filter_status' to 'status' to match backend query parameter.  4. Defect: Dashboard's Article Analytics Button uses static article_id=0 causing 404 Description: On dashboard.html, the Article Analytics button hardcodes article_id=0 which likely does not exist, leading to 404 error when accessed. Steps to Reproduce: - Login and land on dashboard - Click Article Analytics button - Observe 404 error page Suggestion: - Change the Article Analytics nav button to link dynamically to analytics of a valid article,   or disable/replace it with a page listing articles to choose from for analytics.  5. Defect: Article Edit Form Does Not Provide Content Field on GET Description: The edit_article route GET expects to render edit_article.html with article content, but the ArticleModel article dictionary has no 'content' field on article level, content is in versions. Steps to Reproduce: - Login and navigate to edit an article - Observe the content textarea is blank or does not populate Suggestion: - Populate the content textarea with the latest version's content for that article. - Pass latest version content separately to template.  6. Defect: Article Analytics Page Expects traffic_by_date but Backend Does Not Provide Description: The article_analytics.html expects analytics_overview.traffic_by_date to render a table, but backend analytics_overview dict has no such data. Steps to Reproduce: - Login and go to article analytics page - Observe template error for missing 'traffic_by_date' or empty table Suggestion: - Adjust backend to include dummy or real traffic_by_date data in analytics_overview or - Modify template to handle absence gracefully.  7. Defect: Version History Template Has Incorrect Variable Names and Missing Comparison Logic Description: version_history.html template expects 'comparison' variable and fields like version.timestamp, but backend route provides compare_versions list, no comparison variable. Also version dict uses 'created_at' not 'timestamp'. Steps to Reproduce: - Login and open version history page - Observe errors or missing comparison data Suggestion: - Backend route should prepare a comparison dictionary or object for template. - Update template to match version dict keys.  ---  Final Status: NEED_MODIFY  All above defects prevent full functional usage of the application. Providing the missing utils module and aligning template variables to backend data structure, as well as fixing form parameter names and dynamic routing, are essential fixes.

parse_pipe_line(line, fields_count):     parts = line.strip().split('|')     if len(parts) != fields_count:         return None     return [p.strip() for p in parts]   def iso_datetime_now(): === LLM_call: SystemArchitect ===
INPUT: === feedback_testing.txt ===
Feedback for Content Publishing Hub - Functional Testing

1. Defect: Missing utils.file_io Module Causes Import Errors
Description: The backend app.py fails to start due to missing 'utils.file_io' module which provides read_file_lines and write_file_lines_atomic functions. This blocks all functionality since models rely on this module for file IO.
Steps to Reproduce:
- Start or import app.py or any model class
- Observe ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'utils'
Suggestion:
- Provide or restore the utils/file_io.py module containing the required file IO functions.
- Alternatively, replace file IO in models with an existing and accessible implementation.

2. Defect: Templates Reference Nonexistent or Incorrect Attribute Names
Description: Multiple templates reference article or version attributes using incorrect attribute names that do not align with backend dictionary keys.
Examples:
- edit_article.html uses article.id and article.content but backend article dictionary uses 'article_id' and no direct 'content' field.
- my_articles.html uses article.id and article.last_modified, but backend article dict uses 'article_id' and 'updated_at' instead of 'last_modified'.
- published_articles.html references article.id and article.published_date; backend articles have no 'published_date' field.
- article_analytics.html references analytics_overview.total_views and avg_time_on_page but backend provides keys as 'views' and 'avg_read_time_seconds'.
- version_history.html references version.version_id and version.timestamp; backend version dict uses 'version_id' and 'created_at'.
Steps to Reproduce:
- Login and navigate to relevant pages such as Edit Article, My Articles, Published Articles, Analytics, or Version History.
- Observe template rendering errors or missing data display.
Suggestion:
- Align template variable usage with backend dictionary keys, e.g., article.article_id, article.updated_at instead of article.id, article.last_modified.
- Pass additional data if needed, e.g., computed published_date.
- Modify analytics template to use keys 'views' and 'avg_read_time_seconds'.
- Ensure consistent naming conventions.

3. Defect: My Articles Page Filter Form Uses Incorrect Parameter Name
Description: The my_articles route expects a 'status' query parameter for filtering, but my_articles.html filter form submits parameter named 'filter_status'. This mismatch causes filter not to work.
Steps to Reproduce:
- Login and go to /articles/mine
- Apply filter by status and submit
- Observe no filtering occurs
Suggestion:
- Change filter form select name attribute from 'filter_status' to 'status' to match backend query parameter.

4. Defect: Dashboard's Article Analytics Button uses static article_id=0 causing 404
Description: On dashboard.html, the Article Analytics button hardcodes article_id=0 which likely does not exist, leading to 404 error when accessed.
Steps to Reproduce:
- Login and land on dashboard
- Click Article Analytics button
- Observe 404 error page
Suggestion:
- Change the Article Analytics nav button to link dynamically to analytics of a valid article,
  or disable/replace it with a page listing articles to choose from for analytics.

5. Defect: Article Edit Form Does Not Provide Content Field on GET
Description: The edit_article route GET expects to render edit_article.html with article content, but the ArticleModel article dictionary has no 'content' field on article level, content is in versions.
Steps to Reproduce:
- Login and navigate to edit an article
- Observe the content textarea is blank or does not populate
Suggestion:
- Populate the content textarea with the latest version's content for that article.
- Pass latest version content separately to template.

6. Defect: Article Analytics Page Expects traffic_by_date but Backend Does Not Provide
Description: The article_analytics.html expects analytics_overview.traffic_by_date to render a table, but backend analytics_overview dict has no such data.
Steps to Reproduce:
- Login and go to article analytics page
- Observe template error for missing 'traffic_by_date' or empty table
Suggestion:
- Adjust backend to include dummy or real traffic_by_date data in analytics_overview or
- Modify template to handle absence gracefully.

7. Defect: Version History Template Has Incorrect Variable Names and Missing Comparison Logic
Description: version_history.html template expects 'comparison' variable and fields like version.timestamp, but backend route provides compare_versions list, no comparison variable. Also version dict uses 'created_at' not 'timestamp'.
Steps to Reproduce:
- Login and open version history page
- Observe errors or missing comparison data
Suggestion:
- Backend route should prepare a comparison dictionary or object for template.
- Update template to match version dict keys.

---

Final Status: NEED_MODIFY

All above defects prevent full functional usage of the application.
Providing the missing utils module and aligning template variables to backend data structure, as well as fixing form parameter names and dynamic routing, are essential fixes.


=== app.py ===
import os
import threading
import datetime
import functools
from flask import (
    Flask, render_template, request, redirect, url_for, session, abort, flash
)

# Utility for concurrency-safe file IO
from utils.file_io import read_file_lines, write_file_lines_atomic

app = Flask(__name__)
app.secret_key = os.getenv('SECRET_KEY', 'devsecret')

DATA_DIR = 'data'

# Thread lock for safe file read/write
file_lock = threading.Lock()

# ----------------------
# Helper functions
# ----------------------

def login_required(f):
    @functools.wraps(f)
    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
        if 'username' not in session:
            return redirect(url_for('login', next=request.url))
        return f(*args, **kwargs)
    return decorated


def parse_pipe_line(line, fields_count):
    parts = line.strip().split('|')
    if len(parts) != fields_count:
        return None
    return [p.strip() for p in parts]


def iso_datetime_now():
    return datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat()


# ----------------------
# Models
# ----------------------

class UserModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'users.txt')
    FIELDS = 3  # username|password|other_fields...

    @staticmethod
    def get_all_users():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(UserModel.FILENAME)
        users = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, UserModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                username = parts[0]
                password = parts[1]
                users[username] = {'username': username, 'password': password}
        return users

    @staticmethod
    def validate_user(username, password):
        users = UserModel.get_all_users()
        if username in users and users[username]['password'] == password:
            return True
        return False


class ArticleModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'articles.txt')
    FIELDS = 7  # article_id|owner|title|category|status|created_at|updated_at

    @staticmethod
    def read_all():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(ArticleModel.FILENAME)
        articles = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, ArticleModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                article_id, owner, title, category, status, created_at, updated_at = parts
                articles[article_id] = {
                    'article_id': article_id,
                    'owner': owner,
                    'title': title,
                    'category': category,
                    'status': status,
                    'created_at': created_at,
                    'updated_at': updated_at
                }
        return articles

    @staticmethod
    def save_all(articles):
        lines = []
        for a in articles.values():
            line = '|'.join([
                a['article_id'], a['owner'], a['title'], a['category'], a['status'], a['created_at'], a['updated_at']
            ])
            lines.append(line)
        with file_lock:
            write_file_lines_atomic(ArticleModel.FILENAME, lines)

    @staticmethod
    def add_article(owner, title, category='Uncategorized'):
        article_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        created_at = iso_datetime_now()
        new_article = {
            'article_id': article_id,
            'owner': owner,
            'title': title,
            'category': category,
            'status': 'draft',
            'created_at': created_at,
            'updated_at': created_at,
        }
        articles = ArticleModel.read_all()
        articles[article_id] = new_article
        ArticleModel.save_all(articles)
        return new_article

    @staticmethod
    def update_article(article_id, title=None, category=None, status=None):
        articles = ArticleModel.read_all()
        if article_id not in articles:
            return None
        article = articles[article_id]
        updated = False
        if title is not None:
            article['title'] = title
            updated = True
        if category is not None:
            article['category'] = category
            updated = True
        if status is not None:
            article['status'] = status
            updated = True
        if updated:
            article['updated_at'] = iso_datetime_now()
            articles[article_id] = article
            ArticleModel.save_all(articles)
        return article


import uuid

class VersionModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'article_versions.txt')
    FIELDS = 6  # version_id|article_id|version_number|content|created_at|status

    @staticmethod
    def read_all():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(VersionModel.FILENAME)
        versions = {}
        by_article = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, VersionModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                version_id, article_id, version_number, content, created_at, status = parts
                version_number = int(version_number)
                v = {
                    'version_id': version_id,
                    'article_id': article_id,
                    'version_number': version_number,
                    'content': content,
                    'created_at': created_at,
                    'status': status
                }
                versions[version_id] = v
                by_article.setdefault(article_id, []).append(v)
        # sort versions by version_number for each article
        for art_id in by_article:
            by_article[art_id].sort(key=lambda x: x['version_number'])
        return versions, by_article

    @staticmethod
    def save_all(versions):
        # versions is dict by version_id
        lines = []
        for v in versions.values():
            line = '|'.join([
                v['version_id'], v['article_id'], str(v['version_number']), v['content'], v['created_at'], v['status']
            ])
            lines.append(line)
        with file_lock:
            write_file_lines_atomic(VersionModel.FILENAME, lines)

    @staticmethod
    def add_version(article_id, content, status='draft'):
        versions, by_article = VersionModel.read_all()
        existing_versions = by_article.get(article_id, [])
        new_version_number = existing_versions[-1]['version_number'] + 1 if existing_versions else 1
        version_id = f"{article_id}_v{new_version_number}"
        created_at = iso_datetime_now()
        version = {
            'version_id': version_id,
            'article_id': article_id,
            'version_number': new_version_number,
            'content': content,
            'created_at': created_at,
            'status': status
        }
        versions[version_id] = version
        VersionModel.save_all(versions)
        return version

class ApprovalModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'approvals.txt')
    FIELDS = 5  # approval_id|version_id|approver|status|timestamp

    @staticmethod
    def read_all():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(ApprovalModel.FILENAME)
        approvals = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, ApprovalModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                approval_id, version_id, approver, status, timestamp = parts
                approvals[approval_id] = {
                    'approval_id': approval_id,
                    'version_id': version_id,
                    'approver': approver,
                    'status': status,
                    'timestamp': timestamp
                }
        return approvals

    @staticmethod
    def save_all(approvals):
        lines = []
        for a in approvals.values():
            line = '|'.join([
                a['approval_id'], a['version_id'], a['approver'], a['status'], a['timestamp']
            ])
            lines.append(line)
        with file_lock:
            write_file_lines_atomic(ApprovalModel.FILENAME, lines)

    @staticmethod
    def add_approval(version_id, approver, status):
        approvals = ApprovalModel.read_all()
        approval_id = f"{version_id}_{approver}"
        timestamp = iso_datetime_now()
        approvals[approval_id] = {
            'approval_id': approval_id,
            'version_id': version_id,
            'approver': approver,
            'status': status,
            'timestamp': timestamp
        }
        ApprovalModel.save_all(approvals)
        return approvals[approval_id]

class CommentModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'comments.txt')
    FIELDS = 6  # comment_id|article_id|version_id|username|content|created_at

    @staticmethod
    def read_all():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(CommentModel.FILENAME)
        comments = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, CommentModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                comment_id, article_id, version_id, username, content, created_at = parts
                comments[comment_id] = {
                    'comment_id': comment_id,
                    'article_id': article_id,
                    'version_id': version_id,
                    'username': username,
                    'content': content,
                    'created_at': created_at
                }
        return comments

    @staticmethod
    def save_all(comments):
        lines = []
        for c in comments.values():
            line = '|'.join([
                c['comment_id'], c['article_id'], c['version_id'], c['username'], c['content'], c['created_at']
            ])
            lines.append(line)
        with file_lock:
            write_file_lines_atomic(CommentModel.FILENAME, lines)

    @staticmethod
    def add_comment(article_id, version_id, username, content):
        comments = CommentModel.read_all()
        comment_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        created_at = iso_datetime_now()
        comment = {
            'comment_id': comment_id,
            'article_id': article_id,
            'version_id': version_id,
            'username': username,
            'content': content,
            'created_at': created_at
        }
        comments[comment_id] = comment
        CommentModel.save_all(comments)
        return comment

class WorkflowModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'workflow_stages.txt')
    FIELDS = 2  # category|stages (comma separated)

    @staticmethod
    def read_all():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(WorkflowModel.FILENAME)
        workflows = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, WorkflowModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                category, stages_str = parts
                stages = [s.strip() for s in stages_str.split(',') if s.strip()]
                workflows[category] = stages
        return workflows

class AnalyticsModel:
    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'analytics.txt')
    FIELDS = 6  # article_id|version_id|views|unique_visitors|avg_read_time_seconds|shares

    @staticmethod
    def read_all():
        with file_lock:
            lines = read_file_lines(AnalyticsModel.FILENAME)
        analytics = {}
        for line in lines:
            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, AnalyticsModel.FIELDS)
            if parts:
                article_id, version_id, views, uv, art, shares = parts
                analytics_key = (article_id, version_id)
                analytics[analytics_key] = {
                    'views': int(views),
                    'unique_visitors': int(uv),
                    'avg_read_time_seconds': float(art),
                    'shares': int(shares)
                }
        return analytics

# ----------------------
# Services
# ----------------------

from approval_service import ApprovalService
from analytics_service import AnalyticsService
from article_service import ArticleService

# ----------------------
# Routes
# ----------------------

@app.route('/')
def root():
    if 'username' in session:
        return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))
    return redirect(url_for('login'))

@app.route('/login', methods=['GET','POST'])
def login():
    if request.method == 'GET':
        return render_template('login.html')
    username = request.form.get('username','').strip()
    password = request.form.get('password','').strip()
    if not username or not password:
        flash('Username and password required','error')
        return redirect(url_for('login'))
    if UserModel.validate_user(username, password):
        session['username'] = username
        next_url = request.args.get('next') or url_for('dashboard')
        return redirect(next_url)
    flash('Invalid username or password','error')
    return redirect(url_for('login'))

@app.route('/logout')
@login_required
def logout():
    session.pop('username', None)
    flash('Logged out successfully','info')
    return redirect(url_for('login'))

@app.route('/dashboard')
@login_required
def dashboard():
    username = session['username']
    quick_stats = ArticleService.get_quick_stats(username)
    recent_activity = ArticleService.get_recent_activity(username)
    return render_template('dashboard.html', username=username, quick_stats=quick_stats, recent_activity=recent_activity)

@app.route('/article/create', methods=['GET','POST'])
@login_required
def create_article():
    if request.method == 'GET':
        categories = ArticleService.get_all_categories()
        return render_template('create_article.html', categories=categories)
    # POST
    title = request.form.get('title','').strip()
    content = request.form.get('content','').strip()
    category = request.form.get('category','Uncategorized').strip()
    if not title or not content:
        flash('Title and Content are required','error')
        return redirect(url_for('create_article'))
    username = session['username']
    article = ArticleService.create_article(username, title, content, category)
    flash('Article created successfully','success')
    return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article['article_id']))

@app.route('/article/<article_id>/edit', methods=['GET','POST'])
@login_required
def edit_article(article_id):
    username = session['username']
    article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    if article['owner'] != username:
        abort(403)

    if request.method == 'GET':
        versions = VersionModel.read_all()[1].get(article_id, [])
        return render_template('edit_article.html', article=article, versions=versions)

    # POST
    title = request.form.get('title','').strip()
    content = request.form.get('content','').strip()
    if not title or not content:
        flash('Title and Content are required','error')
        return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id))

    # Update article with new title if changed
    ArticleModel.update_article(article_id, title=title)

    # Save new version
    ArticleService.create_version(article_id, content)

    flash('Article updated with new version','success')
    return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id))

@app.route('/article/<article_id>/versions', methods=['GET'])
@login_required
def article_version_history(article_id):
    article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    versions = VersionModel.read_all()[1].get(article_id, [])
    current_version_num = versions[-1]['version_number'] if versions else 0

    # For comparison, demo pass two latest versions
    compare_versions = versions[-2:] if len(versions) > 1 else versions
    return render_template('version_history.html', article=article, versions=versions, compare_versions=compare_versions)

@app.route('/articles/mine', methods=['GET'])
@login_required
def my_articles():
    username = session['username']
    status_filter = request.args.get('status', None)
    all_articles = ArticleModel.read_all()
    filtered = [a for a in all_articles.values() if a['owner'] == username]
    if status_filter:
        filtered = [a for a in filtered if a['status'] == status_filter]
    statuses = set(a['status'] for a in filtered)
    return render_template('my_articles.html', articles=filtered, statuses=sorted(statuses), selected_status=status_filter)

@app.route('/articles/published', methods=['GET'])
def published_articles():
    category_filter = request.args.get('category', None)
    sort_option = request.args.get('sort', 'created_desc')
    all_articles = ArticleModel.read_all()
    published = [a for a in all_articles.values() if a['status'] == 'published']
    if category_filter:
        published = [a for a in published if a['category'] == category_filter]

    # Sorting demo by created_at descending or ascending
    reverse = True if sort_option.endswith('_desc') else False
    key = None
    if 'created' in sort_option:
        key = lambda x: x['created_at']
    elif 'title' in sort_option:
        key = lambda x: x['title'].lower()
    if key:
        published.sort(key=key, reverse=reverse)

    # Extract categories for filter dropdown
    categories = sorted(set(a['category'] for a in all_articles.values()))

    return render_template('published_articles.html', articles=published, categories=categories, selected_category=category_filter, sort_option=sort_option)

@app.route('/calendar', methods=['GET'])
def content_calendar():
    view_mode = request.args.get('view', 'month')
    # For demo, gather publish dates and events
    all_articles = ArticleModel.read_all()
    versions_by_article = VersionModel.read_all()[1]
    scheduled_events = []
    for article in all_articles.values():
        article_id = article['article_id']
        versions = versions_by_article.get(article_id, [])
        # Use latest approved or published version publish date or similar
        for v in reversed(versions):
            if v['status'] in ('approved','published'):
                # Placeholder: assume publish date stored in content (not defined), use created_at
                scheduled_events.append({
                    'article_id': article_id,
                    'title': article['title'],
                    'date': v['created_at']
                })
                break
    return render_template('content_calendar.html', view_mode=view_mode, events=scheduled_events)

@app.route('/article/<article_id>/analytics', methods=['GET'])
@login_required
def article_analytics(article_id):
    article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    versions = VersionModel.read_all()[1].get(article_id, [])
    analytics_all = AnalyticsModel.read_all()

    # Aggregate analytics by summing across versions
    aggregated = {
        'views': 0,
        'unique_visitors': 0,
        'avg_read_time_seconds': 0.0,
        'shares': 0
    }
    count_versions = 0
    for v in versions:
        key = (article_id, v['version_id'])
        a = analytics_all.get(key)
        if a:
            aggregated['views'] += a['views']
            aggregated['unique_visitors'] += a['unique_visitors']
            aggregated['avg_read_time_seconds'] += a['avg_read_time_seconds']
            aggregated['shares'] += a['shares']
            count_versions += 1
    if count_versions > 0:
        aggregated['avg_read_time_seconds'] /= count_versions

    return render_template('article_analytics.html', article=article, analytics_overview=aggregated)

# Approval submit
@app.route('/article/<article_id>/approve', methods=['POST'])
@login_required
def article_approve(article_id):
    username = session['username']
    article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    status = request.form.get('status','').strip()
    if status not in ('approved', 'rejected', 'revision_requested'):
        flash('Invalid approval status','error')
        return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id))

    versions = VersionModel.read_all()[1].get(article_id, [])
    if not versions:
        flash('No versions found to approve','error')
        return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id))
    latest_version = versions[-1]
    version_id = latest_version['version_id']

    ApprovalModel.add_approval(version_id, username, status)

    # Evaluate aggregate approval status
    ApprovalService.update_version_status(article_id, version_id)

    flash('Approval status updated','success')
    return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))

# Add comment
@app.route('/article/<article_id>/comment', methods=['POST'])
@login_required
def article_comment(article_id):
    username = session['username']
    article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)
    if article is None:
        abort(404)
    content = request.form.get('content','').strip()
    version_id = request.form.get('version_id','').strip()
    if not content or not version_id:
        flash('Comment content and version required','error')
        return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))

    CommentModel.add_comment(article_id, version_id, username, content)

    flash('Comment added','success')
    return redirect(url_for('article_version_history', article_id=article_id))


if __name__ == '__main__':
    os.makedirs(DATA_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    app.run(debug=True)

@login_required def article_analytics(article_id):     article = ArticleModel.read_all().get(article_id)     if article is None:         abort(404)     versions = 'unique_visitors': 0,         'avg_read_time_seconds': 0.0,         'shares': 0     }     count_versions = 0     for v in versions:         key = (article_id, v['version_id'])         a = analytics_all.get(key)         if a:             aggregated['views'] += a['views']             aggregated['unique_visitors'] += a['unique_visitors']             aggregated['avg_read_time_seconds'] += a['avg_read_time_seconds']             aggregated['shares'] += a['shares']             count_versions += 1     if count_versions > 0:         aggregated['avg_read_time_seconds'] /= count_versions      return render_template('article_analytics.html', article=article, analytics_overview=aggregated)  # Approval submit @app.route('/article/<article_id>/approve', methods=['POST']) @login_required def   [feedback_testing.txt] Feedback for Content Publishing Hub - Functional Testing  1. Defect: Missing utils.file_io Module Causes Import Errors Description: The backend app.py fails to start due to missing 'utils.file_io' module which provides read_file_lines and write_file_lines_atomic functions. This blocks all functionality since models rely on this module for file IO. Steps to Reproduce: - Start or import app.py or any model class - Observe ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'utils' Suggestion: - Provide or restore the utils/file_io.py module containing the required file IO functions. - Alternatively, replace file IO in models with an existing and accessible implementation.  2. Defect: Templates Reference Nonexistent or Incorrect Attribute Names Description: Multiple templates reference article or version attributes using incorrect attribute names that do not align with backend dictionary keys. Examples: - edit_article.html uses article.id and article.content but backend article dictionary uses 'article_id' and no direct 'content' field. - my_articles.html uses article.id and article.last_modified, but backend article dict uses 'article_id' and 'updated_at' instead of 'last_modified'. - published_articles.html references article.id and article.published_date; backend articles have no 'published_date' field. - article_analytics.html references analytics_overview.total_views and avg_time_on_page but backend provides keys as 'views' and 'avg_read_time_seconds'. - version_history.html references version.version_id and version.timestamp; backend version dict uses 'version_id' and 'created_at'. Steps to Reproduce: - Login and navigate to relevant pages such as Edit Article, My Articles, Published Articles, Analytics, or Version History. - Observe template rendering errors or missing data display. Suggestion: - Align template variable usage with backend dictionary keys, e.g., article.article_id, article.updated_at instead of article.id, article.last_modified. - Pass additional data if needed, e.g., computed published_date. - Modify analytics template to use keys 'views' and 'avg_read_time_seconds'. - Ensure consistent naming conventions.  3. Defect: My Articles Page Filter Form Uses Incorrect Parameter Name Description: The my_articles route expects a 'status' query parameter for filtering, but my_articles.html filter form submits parameter named 'filter_status'. This mismatch causes filter not to work. Steps to Reproduce: - Login and go to /articles/mine - Apply filter by status and submit - Observe no filtering occurs Suggestion: - Change filter form select name attribute from 'filter_status' to 'status' to match backend query parameter.  4. Defect: Dashboard's Article Analytics Button uses static article_id=0 causing 404 Description: On dashboard.html, the Article Analytics button hardcodes article_id=0 which likely does not exist, leading to 404 error when accessed. Steps to Reproduce: - Login and land on dashboard - Click Article Analytics button - Observe 404 error page Suggestion: - Change the Article Analytics nav button to link dynamically to analytics of a valid article,   or disable/replace it with a page listing articles to choose from for analytics.  5. Defect: Article Edit Form Does Not Provide Content Field on GET Description: The edit_article route GET expects to render edit_article.html with article content, but the ArticleModel article dictionary has no 'content' field on article level, content is in versions. Steps to Reproduce: - Login and navigate to edit an article - Observe the content textarea is blank or does not populate Suggestion: - Populate the content textarea with the latest version's content for that article. - Pass latest version content separately to template.  6. Defect: Article Analytics Page Expects traffic_by_date but Backend Does Not Provide Description: The article_analytics.html expects analytics_overview.traffic_by_date to render a table, but backend analytics_overview dict has no such data. Steps to Reproduce: - Login and go to article analytics page - Observe template error for missing 'traffic_by_date' or empty table Suggestion: - Adjust backend to include dummy or real traffic_by_date data in analytics_overview or - Modify template to handle absence gracefully.  7. Defect: Version History Template Has Incorrect Variable Names and Missing Comparison Logic Description: version_history.html template expects 'comparison' variable and fields like version.timestamp, but backend route provides compare_versions list, no comparison variable. Also version dict uses 'created_at' not 'timestamp'. Steps to Reproduce: - Login and open version history page - Observe errors or missing comparison data Suggestion: - Backend route should prepare a comparison dictionary or object for template. - Update template to match version dict keys.  ---  Final Status: NEED_MODIFY  All above defects prevent full functional usage of the application. Providing the missing utils module and aligning template variables to backend data structure, as well as fixing form parameter names and dynamic routing, are essential fixes.

= Flask(__name__)\napp.secret_key = os.getenv('SECRET_KEY', 'devsecret')\n\nDATA_DIR = 'data'\n\n# Thread lock for safe file read/write\nfile_lock = threading.Lock()\n\n# ----------------------\n# Helper functions\n# ----------------------\n\ndef login_required(f):\n    @functools.wraps(f)\n    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):\n        if 'username' not in session:\n            return redirect(url_for('login', next=request.url))\n        return f(*args, **kwargs)\n    return decorated\n\n\ndef parse_pipe_line(line, fields_count):\n    parts = line.strip().split('|')\n    if len(parts) != fields_count:\n        return None\n    return [p.strip() for p in parts]\n\n\ndef iso_datetime_now():\n    return datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n\n\n# ----------------------\n# Models\n# ----------------------\n\nclass UserModel:\n    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'users.txt')\n    FIELDS = 3  # username|password|other_fields...\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_all_users():\n        with file_lock:\n            lines = read_file_lines(UserModel.FILENAME)\n        users = {}\n        for line in lines:\n            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, UserModel.FIELDS)\n            if parts:\n                username = parts[0]\n                password = parts[1]\n                users[username] = {'username': username, 'password': password}\n        return users\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def validate_user(username, password):\n        users = UserModel.get_all_users()\n        if username in users and users[username]['password'] == password:\n            return True\n        return False\n\n\nclass ArticleModel:\n    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'articles.txt')\n    FIELDS = 7  # article_id|owner|title|category|status|created_at|updated_at\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def read_all():\n        with file_lock:\n            lines = read_file_lines(ArticleModel.FILENAME)\n        articles = {}\n        for line in lines:\n            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, ArticleModel.FIELDS)\n            if parts:\n                article_id, owner, title, category, status, created_at, updated_at = parts\n                articles[article_id] = {\n                    'article_id': article_id,\n                    'owner': owner,\n                    'title': title,\n                    'category': category,\n                    'status': status,\n                    'created_at': created_at,\n                    'updated_at': updated_at\n                }\n        return articles\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def save_all(articles):\n        lines = []\n        for a in articles.values():\n            line = '|'.join([\n                a['article_id'], a['owner'], a['title'], a['category'], a['status'], a['created_at'], a['updated_at']\n            ])\n            lines.append(line)\n        with file_lock:\n            write_file_lines_atomic(ArticleModel.FILENAME, lines)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def add_article(owner, title, category='Uncategorized'):\n        article_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n        created_at = iso_datetime_now()\n        new_article = {\n            'article_id': article_id,\n            'owner': owner,\n            'title': title,\n            'category': category,\n            'status': 'draft',\n            'created_at': created_at,\n            'updated_at': created_at,\n        }\n        articles = ArticleModel.read_all()\n        articles[article_id] = new_article\n        ArticleModel.save_all(articles)\n        return new_article\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def update_article(article_id, title=None, category=None, status=None):\n        articles = ArticleModel.read_all()\n        if article_id not in articles:\n            return None\n        article = articles[article_id]\n        updated = False\n        if title is not None:\n            article['title'] = title\n            updated = True\n        if category is not None:\n            article['category'] = category\n            updated = True\n        if status is not None:\n            article['status'] = status\n            updated = True\n        if updated:\n            article['updated_at'] = iso_datetime_now()\n            articles[article_id] = article\n            ArticleModel.save_all(articles)\n        return article\n\n\nclass VersionModel:\n    FILENAME = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'article_versions.txt')\n    FIELDS = 6  # version_id|article_id|version_number|content|created_at|status\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def read_all():\n        with file_lock:\n            lines = read_file_lines(VersionModel.FILENAME)\n        versions = {}\n        by_article = {}\n        for line in lines:\n            parts = parse_pipe_line(line, VersionModel.FIELDS)\n            if parts:\n                version_id, article_id, version_number, content, created_at, status = parts\n                version_number = int(version_number)\n                v = {\n                    'version_id': version_id,\n                    'article_id': article_id,\n                    'version_number': version_number,\n                    'content': content,\n                    'created_at': created_at,\n                    'status': status\n                }\n                versions[version_id] = v\n                by_article.setdefault(article_id, []).append(v)\n        # sort versions by version_number for each article\n        for art_id in by_article:\n            by_article[art_id].sort(key=lambda x: x['version_number'])\n        return versions, by_article\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def save_all(versions):\n        # versions is dict by version_id\n        lines = []\n        for v in versions.values():\n            line = '|'.join([\n                v['version_id'], v['article_id'], str(v['version_number']), v['content'], v['created_at'], v['status']\n            ])\n            lines.append(line)\n        with file_lock:\n            write_file_lines_atomic(VersionModel.FILENAME, lines)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def add_version(article_id, content, status='draft'):\n        versions, by_article = VersionModel.read_all()\n        existing_versions = by_article.get(article_id, [])\n        new_version_number = existing_versions[-1]['version_number'] + 1 if existing_versions else 1\n        version_id = f\"{article_id}_v{new_version_number}\"\n        created_at = iso_datetime_now()\n        version = {\n            'version_id': version_id,\n            'article_id': article_id,\n            'version_number': new_version_number,\n            'content': content,\n            'created_at': created_at,\n            'status': status\n        }\n        versions[version_id] = version\n        VersionModel.save_all(versions)\n        return version\n\nclass ApprovalModel:\n
